This is not "just an example" - it is the proof that ftui works for its primary use case: Claude Code / Codex-style agent UIs. ## Purpose The agent harness reference application demonstrates: 1. Inline mode with streaming logs and stable UI chrome 2. No flicker, no cursor corruption, reliable cleanup 3. Real-world patterns for agent harness builders ## Minimum Harness Primitives (from Plan 0.8.3) - `write_log(text)` - scrollback-native (inline mode) - `present_ui(frame)` - atomic and flicker-resistant - Optional "modal alt-screen" for complex UI interactions - Child-process capture via PTY (feature gated) ## Application Structure ``` ftui-harness/ src/ main.rs # Entry point app.rs # Application model components/ log_viewer.rs # Scrolling log display status_bar.rs # Top status line tool_status.rs # Tool execution indicator input_line.rs # User input/prompt commands.rs # Command handling pty_capture.rs # Feature-gated PTY support examples/ minimal.rs # Bare minimum harness streaming.rs # High-volume log streaming modal.rs # Modal alt-screen demo ``` ## UI Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Status] Model: claude-3 │ Tool: Running grep... │ Memory: 2.1GB │ 23:45│ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Searching for patterns in codebase... │ │ Found 42 matches in src/ │ │ Analyzing results... │ │ [more log output continues...] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ > Enter command: [⠋]│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Regions - **Status Bar (1 line)**: Model info, tool status, memory, time - **Log Region (variable)**: Scrolling log viewer, grows with terminal - **Input Region (1-2 lines)**: User prompt + spinner ## Application Model ```rust use ftui::{App, Cmd, Event, Frame, Model, ScreenMode, Result}; pub struct AgentHarness { // UI Components log_viewer: LogViewer, status_bar: StatusBar, input_line: InputLine, spinner: Spinner, // State model_name: String, current_tool: Option, memory_usage: u64, // Mode screen_mode: ScreenMode, ui_height: u16, } impl Model for AgentHarness { type Message = HarnessMessage; fn init(&mut self) -> Cmd { Cmd::batch(vec![ Cmd::tick(Duration::from_millis(100)), // Spinner animation Cmd::tick(Duration::from_secs(1)), // Status refresh ]) } fn update(&mut self, msg: HarnessMessage) -> Cmd { match msg { HarnessMessage::LogLine(line) => { self.log_viewer.push(line); Cmd::none() } HarnessMessage::ToolStart(name) => { self.current_tool = Some(name); Cmd::none() } HarnessMessage::ToolEnd => { self.current_tool = None; Cmd::none() } HarnessMessage::Key(key) => { self.handle_key(key) } HarnessMessage::Tick => { self.spinner.tick(); Cmd::none() } HarnessMessage::Quit => Cmd::quit(), } } fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) { let area = frame.buffer.area(); // Status bar at top let status_area = Rect::new(0, 0, area.width, 1); self.status_bar.render(status_area, &mut frame.buffer); // Log viewer in middle let log_area = Rect::new(0, 1, area.width, area.height - self.ui_height); self.log_viewer.render(log_area, &mut frame.buffer); // Input line at bottom let input_area = Rect::new(0, area.height - 2, area.width, 2); self.input_line.render(input_area, &mut frame.buffer); // Spinner in corner let spinner_area = Rect::new(area.width - 2, area.height - 1, 2, 1); if self.current_tool.is_some() { self.spinner.render(spinner_area, &mut frame.buffer); } } } ``` ## Key Features to Demonstrate ### 1. Streaming Log Output ```rust // Simulate tool output arriving in chunks async fn stream_tool_output(tx: Sender) { let output = run_tool().await; for line in output.lines() { tx.send(HarnessMessage::LogLine(line.to_string())).await?; // Log lines appear smoothly without flicker } } ``` ### 2. Inline Mode Stability ```rust fn main() -> Result<()> { App::new(AgentHarness::new()) .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 4 }) .run() // Native scrollback preserved // UI never corrupts scrollback // Cursor returns to correct position } ``` ### 3. Modal Alt-Screen (Optional) ```rust impl AgentHarness { fn enter_file_picker(&mut self) -> Cmd { // Temporarily switch to alt-screen for full-screen picker Cmd::batch(vec![ Cmd::enter_alt_screen(), Cmd::msg(HarnessMessage::ShowPicker), ]) } fn exit_file_picker(&mut self) -> Cmd { Cmd::batch(vec![ Cmd::exit_alt_screen(), Cmd::msg(HarnessMessage::PickerClosed), ]) } } ``` ### 4. Panic Recovery ```rust // Panic anywhere should restore terminal std::panic::set_hook(Box::new(|info| { // Terminal cleanup happens via RAII guards eprintln!("Panic: {}", info); })); ``` ### 5. PTY Subprocess Capture (Feature-Gated) ```rust #[cfg(feature = "pty")] fn run_subprocess_with_capture() { let (master, slave) = openpty()?; let child = Command::new("cargo") .args(["test"]) .stdin(slave) .stdout(slave) .stderr(slave) .spawn()?; // Read from master, forward to log viewer // All output goes through ftui (one-writer rule preserved) } ``` ## Tests Required ### Unit Tests - [ ] Model state transitions - [ ] Command generation - [ ] Log viewer updates - [ ] Status bar formatting ### Integration Tests - [ ] Full app lifecycle (start → use → exit) - [ ] Resize handling - [ ] Log streaming at high volume - [ ] Modal transitions ### PTY Tests - [ ] Inline mode cursor contract - [ ] Scrollback preservation - [ ] Cleanup on exit - [ ] Cleanup on panic - [ ] No flicker under load ### Performance Tests - [ ] 60 FPS with 1000 log lines - [ ] Smooth scrolling - [ ] Low latency input response ## Documentation The harness should be WELL DOCUMENTED as it serves as the primary example: - Inline comments explaining patterns - README with usage instructions - Architecture diagram ## Acceptance Criteria (from Plan 17.4) - [ ] "Hello world harness" in < 200 LOC - [ ] "Tool output streaming" is one function call - [ ] "Pinned bottom UI" is one config option - [ ] "Temporary full-screen modal" is supported without rewriting - [ ] No flicker under sustained log output - [ ] PTY tests pass (cursor, cleanup, scrollback) ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-29v (LogViewer widget) - Requires: bd-2dc (Status/Panel widgets) - Requires: bd-gpe (TextInput widget) - Requires: bd-35p {"id":"bd-2ky9","title":"E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging ## Background Plan Section Testing + Verification requires end-to-end tests that validate the complete pipeline from user input to terminal output. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging." ## E2E Test Philosophy E2E tests should: 1. Test the complete system as a user would experience it 2. Run in a PTY to capture real terminal behavior 3. Log every step for post-mortem debugging 4. Be deterministic and reproducible 5. Cover happy paths and error cases ## E2E Test Script Structure ### Directory Layout ``` tests/ e2e/ fixtures/ # Test input files expected/ # Expected output snapshots scripts/ # Test runner scripts run_all.sh # Master test runner test_inline.sh # Inline mode tests test_altscreen.sh # Alt-screen tests test_cleanup.sh # Cleanup/panic tests test_input.sh # Input handling tests lib/ common.sh # Shared utilities logging.sh # Logging functions pty.sh # PTY spawning helpers ``` ### Logging Framework (logging.sh) ```bash #!/bin/bash # Comprehensive logging for E2E tests LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-DEBUG} LOG_FILE=${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/ftui_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log} log() { local level=$1 shift local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N") local caller="${BASH_SOURCE[2]}:${BASH_LINENO[1]}" echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" } log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "DEBUG" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$@"; } log_info() { log "INFO" "$@"; } log_warn() { log "WARN" "$@"; } log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; } log_test_start() { local test_name=$1 log_info "==========================================" log_info "STARTING TEST: $test_name" log_info "==========================================" } log_test_pass() { local test_name=$1 log_info "✓ PASSED: $test_name" } log_test_fail() { local test_name=$1 local reason=$2 log_error "✗ FAILED: $test_name" log_error " Reason: $reason" log_error " Log file: $LOG_FILE" } log_hex_dump() { local label=$1 local data=$2 log_debug "$label (hex): $(echo -n "$data" | xxd -p | tr -d n)" } ``` ## E2E Test Categories ### 1. Inline Mode Tests (test_inline.sh) Tests that streaming logs + UI chrome works correctly. ```bash test_inline_log_scroll() { log_test_start "Inline mode: log scrolling" # Setup local pty_output=$(mktemp) log_debug "PTY output file: $pty_output" # Run ftui app that streams 100 log lines with 6-line UI log_info "Spawning ftui inline mode app..." timeout 10s script -q -c "./target/debug/ftui-harness --mode inline --ui-height 6 --log-count 100" "$pty_output" local exit_code=$? log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code" # Verify log_info "Verifying output..." # Check scrollback preservation if grep -q "Log line 1" "$pty_output"; then log_debug "Early log lines found in scrollback ✓" else log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "Early log lines missing from scrollback" return 1 fi # Check UI region intact if tail -6 "$pty_output" | grep -q "\[Status\]"; then log_debug "UI region present at bottom ✓" else log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "UI region not at expected position" return 1 fi log_test_pass "Inline mode: log scrolling" rm "$pty_output" } ``` ### 2. Cleanup Tests (test_cleanup.sh) Tests that terminal is restored after exit/panic. ```bash test_panic_cleanup() { log_test_start "Panic cleanup: terminal restored" # Capture terminal state before local before_state=$(stty -g) log_debug "Terminal state before: $before_state" # Run app that panics log_info "Running app that will panic..." ./target/debug/ftui-panic-test 2>/dev/null || true # Capture terminal state after local after_state=$(stty -g) log_debug "Terminal state after: $after_state" # Verify restoration if [[ "$before_state" == "$after_state" ]]; then log_debug "Terminal state restored ✓" else log_test_fail "Panic cleanup" "Terminal state not restored" log_error " Before: $before_state" log_error " After: $after_state" return 1 fi # Check cursor visible (SGR sequence check) log_info "Checking cursor visibility..." # ... additional checks ... log_test_pass "Panic cleanup: terminal restored" } ``` ### 3. Input Tests (test_input.sh) Tests for keyboard, mouse, paste handling. ### 4. ANSI Output Tests Verify presenter output matches expected sequences. ## Test Output Format Each test run produces: 1. Console output (PASS/FAIL per test) 2. Detailed log file with timestamps 3. JSON summary for CI 4. PTY captures for failed tests ### JSON Summary Format ```json { "timestamp": "2026-01-31T23:45:00Z", "total": 50, "passed": 48, "failed": 2, "skipped": 0, "duration_ms": 12345, "tests": [ { "name": "test_inline_log_scroll", "status": "passed", "duration_ms": 234, "log_lines": 45 }, { "name": "test_panic_cleanup", "status": "failed", "duration_ms": 567, "error": "Terminal state not restored", "log_file": "/tmp/ftui_e2e_20260131_234500.log" } ] } ``` ## E2E Test Inventory | Test | Description | Priority | |------|-------------|----------| | inline_basic | Basic inline mode display | P0 | | inline_log_scroll | Log scrolling preserves scrollback | P0 | | inline_resize | Resize re-anchors UI | P0 | | inline_cursor | Cursor position contract | P0 | | altscreen_enter_exit | Alt-screen lifecycle | P0 | | cleanup_normal | Terminal restored on exit | P0 | | cleanup_panic | Terminal restored on panic | P0 | | cleanup_io_error | Terminal restored on IO error | P1 | | input_key_basic | Basic key parsing | P0 | | input_key_modifiers | Modifier key detection | P1 | | input_mouse_click | Mouse click handling | P1 | | input_mouse_scroll | Mouse scroll handling | P1 | | input_paste | Bracketed paste handling | P1 | | input_focus | Focus events | P2 | | ansi_sgr | SGR sequence output | P0 | | ansi_osc8 | OSC 8 hyperlink output | P1 | | ansi_sync | Synchronized output | P1 | | unicode_basic | ASCII width handling | P0 | | unicode_wide | CJK wide chars | P0 | | unicode_emoji | Emoji rendering | P0 | | unicode_zwj | ZWJ sequences | P1 | ## Acceptance Criteria - This is important for inline mode to work correctly under multiplexers. ## Why This Matters - tmux/screen intercept escape sequences - Some features (sync output, OSC 8) need passthrough - Scroll regions behave differently under muxes - Detection enables correct fallback strategies ## Technical Design ### Detection ```rust impl TerminalCapabilities { pub fn detect() -> Self { // Multiplexer detection let in_tmux = std::env::var("TMUX").is_ok(); let in_screen = std::env::var("STY").is_ok(); let in_zellij = std::env::var("ZELLIJ").is_ok(); // Note: WezTerm/Kitty built-in muxes don't need special handling Self { in_tmux, in_screen, in_zellij, // ... } } pub fn in_any_mux(&self) -> bool { self.in_tmux || self.in_screen || self.in_zellij } } ``` ### tmux Passthrough Wrapper ```rust /// Wrap sequence for tmux passthrough /// tmux intercepts escape sequences unless wrapped in DCS passthrough pub fn tmux_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String { if in_tmux() { // Double escapes for tmux let escaped = sequence.replace('\\x1b', "\\x1b\\x1b"); format\!("\\x1bPtmux;{}\\x1b\\\\", escaped) } else { sequence.to_string() } } // Usage example for OSC 8 hyperlinks under tmux fn emit_hyperlink(url: &str) -> String { let osc = format\!("\\x1b]8;;{}\\x1b\\\\", url); tmux_wrap(&osc) } ``` ### Mux-Aware Feature Policies ```rust impl TerminalCapabilities { /// Returns whether sync output should be enabled pub fn use_sync_output(&self) -> bool { // Disable in multiplexers (passthrough unreliable) if self.in_any_mux() { return false; } self.sync_output } /// Returns whether scroll-region optimization is safe pub fn use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool { // Conservative in muxes due to quirks if self.in_any_mux() { return false; // Fall back to overlay redraw } self.scroll_region } /// OSC 8 hyperlink support pub fn use_hyperlinks(&self) -> bool { // tmux passthrough needed for hyperlinks // Only enable if passthrough is reliable if self.in_tmux { // Modern tmux (3.3+) supports OSC 8 passthrough // but detection is fragile return false; // Conservative default } self.osc8_hyperlinks } } ``` ### Screen Passthrough ```rust /// screen uses different passthrough syntax pub fn screen_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String { if in_screen() { // screen uses P escape sequence format\!("\\x1bP{}\\x1b\\\\", sequence) } else { sequence.to_string() } } ``` ### Zellij Handling ```rust // Zellij generally has better passthrough than tmux/screen // but some features still need care impl TerminalCapabilities { pub fn zellij_passthrough_mode(&self) -> bool { // Zellij 0.39+ has improved passthrough self.in_zellij } } ``` ## Integration Points 1. **TerminalCapabilities::detect()** - Detect multiplexer presence 2. **Presenter output** - Apply passthrough wrapping where needed 3. 13.1 and J specifically require property tests to ensure diff correctness and presenter output validation. Property tests use randomized inputs to find edge cases humans miss. ## Property Test Categories ### Category 1: Diff Correctness **Property**: Applying diff changes to old buffer produces new buffer ```rust proptest! { #[test] fn diff_apply_produces_target( width in 10u16..200, height in 5u16..100, changes in proptest::collection::vec( (0u16..200, 0u16..100, any::()), 0..1000 ) ) { // Create old buffer let mut old = Buffer::new(width, height); // Apply random changes to create new buffer let mut new = old.clone(); for (x, y, cell) in &changes { if *x < width && *y < height { new.set(*x, *y, cell.clone()); } } // Compute diff let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&old, &new); // Apply diff to old should produce new let mut result = old.clone(); for (x, y) in diff.changes() { let cell = new.get(*x, *y).unwrap().clone(); result.set(*x, *y, cell); } // Verify buffers match for y in 0..height { for x in 0..width { prop_assert_eq!( result.get(x, y), new.get(x, y), "Mismatch at ({}, {})", x, y ); } } } } ``` ### Category 2: Presenter Roundtrip **Property**: Presenter output applied to terminal model matches source buffer ```rust proptest! { #[test] fn presenter_roundtrip_correct( width in 10u16..80, height in 5u16..40, fill_ratio in 0.0f32..1.0, ) { // Create random buffer let buffer = generate_random_buffer(width, height, fill_ratio); // Present to bytes let mut output = Vec::new(); let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities::basic()); presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &LinkRegistry::new()) .unwrap(); // Apply to terminal model let mut model = TerminalModel::new(width, height); model.apply_output(&output).unwrap(); // Verify match prop_assert!( model.matches(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new()), "Presenter output doesn't reproduce buffer" ); } } ``` ### Category 3: Style State Never Leaks **Property**: After complete frame, style is reset ```rust proptest! { #[test] fn style_state_reset_after_present( width in 10u16..80, height in 5u16..40, ) { let buffer = generate_styled_buffer(width, height); let mut output = Vec::new(); let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities::basic()); presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &LinkRegistry::new()) .unwrap(); // Output must end with SGR 0 (reset) let output_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output); prop_assert!( output_str.ends_with("\x1b[0m") || output_str.contains("\x1b[0m\x1b["), // Reset before final positioning "Style not reset at end: {}", &output_str[output_str.len().saturating_sub(50)..] ); } } ``` ### Category 4: Links Properly Closed **Property**: Every OSC 8 link open has corresponding close ```rust proptest! { #[test] fn links_balanced(width in 10u16..80, height in 5u16..40) { let buffer = generate_buffer_with_links(width, height); let mut output = Vec::new(); let mut presenter = Presenter::new(&mut output, TerminalCapabilities { osc8_hyperlinks: true, ..TerminalCapabilities::basic() }); presenter.present_full(&buffer, &GraphemePool::new(), &links()) .unwrap(); let output_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output); // Count link opens and closes let opens = output_str.matches("\x1b]8;;").count() // link with URL + output_str.matches("\x1b]8;id=").count(); // link with id let closes = output_str.matches("\x1b]8;;\x1b\\").count(); // Every open needs a close (or one final close) prop_assert!( opens <= closes, "Unbalanced links: {} opens, {} closes", opens, closes ); } } ``` ### Category 5: Width Invariants **Property**: Rendering never writes beyond buffer bounds ```rust proptest! { #[test] fn render_within_bounds( width in 10u16..80, height in 5u16..40, text in ".*", ) { let mut buffer = Buffer::new(width, height); // Render text into buffer (via whatever rendering API) render_text(&mut buffer, &text, 0, 0, width); // Check no writes beyond bounds // (Buffer should enforce this internally, but verify) for y in 0..height { let mut x = 0; while x < width { let cell = buffer.get(x, y).unwrap(); let cell_width = cell.width() as u16; // Wide char can't overflow right edge prop_assert!( x + cell_width <= width, "Wide char at ({}, {}) overflows (width {})", x, y, cell_width ); x += cell_width.max(1); } } } } ``` ## Test Helpers Needed - `generate_random_buffer(w, h, fill_ratio)` - Random content - `generate_styled_buffer(w, h)` - Random styles - `generate_buffer_with_links(w, h)` - Random hyperlinks - `Arbitrary` impl for Cell, CellContent, CellAttrs, PackedRgba ## Running Property Tests ```bash # Run with many iterations PROPTEST_CASES=10000 cargo test --test prop_tests # Generate regression cases on failure PROPTEST_ENABLE_FAILURE_PERSISTENCE=1 cargo test ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Diff apply property test passes 10k iterations - [ ] Presenter roundtrip property test passes 10k iterations - [ ] Style reset property test passes 10k iterations - [ ] Link balance property test passes 10k iterations - [ ] Width bounds property test passes 10k iterations - [ ] No panics under property testing - [ ] Regression cases saved for failed tests ## Location `tests/prop_tests.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:21:15.739827546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:13:12.586774437Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:13:12.586738870Z","close_reason":"Added 10 property tests: 5 for diff (apply produces target, identical empty, only real changes, runs contiguous, runs cover all), 5 for presenter (roundtrip chars, style reset, empty diff minimal, diff bounds, cursor consistency). This is critical for minimal ANSI output and flicker reduction. ## Why This Matters Without run grouping: - Each changed cell requires a cursor move: `\x1b[y;xH` - Each cell change might reset style: `\x1b[0m...` - Output is O(changes × escape_overhead) - Slow terminals show visible tearing With run grouping: - Consecutive changed cells emit one cursor move - Style only changes when cell style differs - Output is O(runs × escape_overhead) - Smooth, fast rendering ## Technical Design ### ChangeRun Structure ```rust /// A contiguous run of changed cells on a single row pub struct ChangeRun { pub y: u16, // Row pub x0: u16, // Start column (inclusive) pub x1: u16, // End column (inclusive) } ``` ### Run Grouping Algorithm ```rust impl BufferDiff { /// Convert point changes into row-major runs pub fn runs(&self, width: u16) -> Vec { if self.changes.is_empty() { return Vec::new(); } // Sort by (y, x) for row-major order let mut sorted: Vec<_> = self.changes.iter().copied().collect(); sorted.sort_by_key(|(x, y)| (*y, *x)); let mut runs = Vec::new(); let mut i = 0; while i < sorted.len() { let (x0, y) = sorted[i]; let mut x1 = x0; i += 1; // Coalesce consecutive x positions on same row while i < sorted.len() { let (x, yy) = sorted[i]; if yy != y || x != x1 + 1 { break; } x1 = x; i += 1; } runs.push(ChangeRun { y, x0, x1 }); } runs } } ``` ### Style-Run Coalescing Within a run, group cells by style to minimize SGR changes: ```rust impl Presenter { fn emit_run( &mut self, run: &ChangeRun, buffer: &Buffer, pool: &GraphemePool, links: &LinkRegistry, ) -> io::Result<()> { // Single cursor move for entire run self.move_cursor_to(run.x0, run.y)?; // Emit cells, tracking style changes for x in run.x0..=run.x1 { let cell = buffer.get_unchecked(x, run.y); if cell.is_continuation() { continue; // Skip wide char continuations } // Only emit style change if different from current self.emit_style_if_changed(cell)?; self.emit_link_if_changed(cell, links)?; self.emit_content(cell, pool)?; // Cursor advances naturally after character output self.cursor_x += cell.width() as u16; } Ok(()) } fn emit_style_if_changed(&mut self, cell: &Cell) -> io::Result<()> { let new_style = CellStyle { fg: cell.fg, bg: cell.bg, attrs: cell.attrs.flags(), }; if Some(new_style) == self.current_style { return Ok(()); // No change needed } // Emit minimal SGR change // v1: reset+apply (simple, correct) // future: incremental (fewer bytes) self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[0")?; It bundles: 1. The cell grid (Buffer) 2. Optional hit testing grid (HitGrid) 3. Cursor metadata ## Structure ```rust /// Frame = Buffer + metadata for a render pass /// /// # Design Rationale /// Frame is passed to Model::view() and contains everything needed to render. /// The Buffer holds cells; metadata controls cursor and enables mouse hit testing. /// /// Frame does NOT own pools (GraphemePool, LinkRegistry) - those are passed separately /// or accessed via RenderContext to allow sharing across frames. pub struct Frame { /// The cell grid for this render pass pub buffer: Buffer, /// Optional hit grid for mouse hit testing /// When Some, widgets can register clickable regions pub hit_grid: Option, /// Cursor position (if app wants to show cursor) /// Coordinates are relative to buffer (0-indexed) pub cursor_position: Option<(u16, u16)>, /// Whether cursor should be visible pub cursor_visible: bool, } ``` ## Frame Lifecycle ```rust impl Frame { /// Create a new frame with given dimensions pub fn new(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self { Self { buffer: Buffer::new(width, height), hit_grid: None, cursor_position: None, cursor_visible: true, } } /// Create frame with hit testing enabled pub fn with_hit_grid(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self { Self { buffer: Buffer::new(width, height), hit_grid: Some(HitGrid::new(width, height)), cursor_position: None, cursor_visible: true, } } /// Dimensions pub fn width(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.width() } pub fn height(&self) -> u16 { self.buffer.height() } /// Clear frame for next render pub fn clear(&mut self) { self.buffer.clear(); if let Some(ref mut grid) = self.hit_grid { grid.clear(); } } /// Set cursor position (None to hide) pub fn set_cursor(&mut self, position: Option<(u16, u16)>) { self.cursor_position = position; } /// Show/hide cursor pub fn set_cursor_visible(&mut self, visible: bool) { self.cursor_visible = visible; } } ``` ## Integration Points ### With Model::view() ```rust trait Model { fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame); } impl Model for MyApp { fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) { // Draw widgets to frame.buffer // Register hit regions with frame.hit_grid // Set cursor position if needed self.status_bar.render(frame); frame.set_cursor(Some((self.cursor_x, self.cursor_y))); } } ``` ### With Presenter ```rust // Runtime uses Frame for diff and presentation let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&prev_buffer, &frame.buffer); presenter.present(&frame.buffer, &diff, &pool, &links)?; // Handle cursor separately if frame.cursor_visible { if let Some((x, y)) = frame.cursor_position { presenter.position_cursor(x, y)?; } presenter.show_cursor()?; } else { presenter.hide_cursor()?; } ``` ## HitGrid (Optional Feature) Hit grid enables mouse interaction by mapping screen positions to widget IDs. ```rust /// Hit testing grid for mouse interaction pub struct HitGrid { width: u16, height: u16, cells: Vec>, } /// Identifier for a clickable region #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)] pub struct HitId(pub u32); impl HitGrid { pub fn register(&mut self, rect: Rect, id: HitId) { for y in rect.y..(rect.y + rect.height) { for x in rect.x..(rect.x + rect.width) { if let Some(cell) = self.get_mut(x, y) { *cell = Some(id); } } } } pub fn hit_test(&self, x: u16, y: u16) -> Option { self.get(x, y).and_then(|c| *c) } } ``` ## Tests Required ```rust #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; #[test] fn test_frame_creation() { let frame = Frame::new(80, 24); assert_eq\!(frame.width(), 80); assert_eq\!(frame.height(), 24); assert\!(frame.hit_grid.is_none()); assert\!(frame.cursor_position.is_none()); assert\!(frame.cursor_visible); } #[test] fn test_frame_with_hit_grid() { let frame = Frame::with_hit_grid(80, 24); assert\!(frame.hit_grid.is_some()); } #[test] fn test_frame_cursor() { let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24); frame.set_cursor(Some((10, 5))); assert_eq\!(frame.cursor_position, Some((10, 5))); frame.set_cursor_visible(false); assert\!(\!frame.cursor_visible); } #[test] fn test_hit_grid_registration() { let mut frame = Frame::with_hit_grid(80, 24); let hit_id = HitId(42); let rect = Rect::new(10, 5, 20, 3); if let Some(ref mut grid) = frame.hit_grid { grid.register(rect, hit_id); // Inside rect assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(15, 6), Some(hit_id)); // Outside rect assert_eq\!(grid.hit_test(5, 5), None); } } } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Frame holds Buffer + optional HitGrid - [ ] Cursor position and visibility tracked - [ ] HitGrid registration and hit testing work - [ ] clear() resets both buffer and hit grid - [ ] Tests pass ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer implementation) - Blocks: bd-10i.8.2 (Runtime uses Frame)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:49:50.364819793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:43:13.013262397Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:43:13.013233763Z","close_reason":"Frame struct fully implemented with HitGrid, tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cc","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:53:47.983923493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3cc","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:53:47.602013413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"bd-3coz","title":"Audit all Widget implementations and categorize by migration complexity","description":"## Purpose Before migrating, we need a complete inventory of all Widget/StatefulWidget implementations and categorize them by how they use the buffer parameter. ## Categories ### Category A: Simple buffer-only widgets These just write to the buffer and don't need any Frame features. // Write header... } pub fn record_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) { let time = self.start_time.elapsed().as_secs_f64(); let event = json!([time, "o", String::from_utf8_lossy(data)]); writeln!(self.output, "{}", event).ok(); } pub fn finish(self) -> io::Result<()> { self.output.flush() } } ``` ## Logging Requirements ```rust // Recording start (INFO level) tracing::info!(path = ?path, width = width, height = height, "Asciicast recording started"); // Output recorded (TRACE level) tracing::trace!(bytes = data.len(), elapsed_secs = time, "Output recorded"); // Recording complete (INFO level) tracing::info!(path = ?path, duration_secs = duration, events = event_count, "Asciicast recording complete"); ``` ## E2E Tests ```bash # Record a session ./ftui-harness --record-asciicast session.cast --duration 10s # Validate asciicast format asciinema cat session.cast > /dev/null && echo "Valid format" # Verify playback asciinema play session.cast --speed 2 # Test with asciinema-player web component ./scripts/test-asciicast-player.sh session.cast ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Asciicast v2 format output (NDJSON) - [ ] Plays correctly in asciinema-player - [ ] Timing preserved accurately (sub-ms precision) - [ ] Works with large recordings (1hr+) - [ ] Integration with ftui runtime - [ ] Input events optionally recorded - [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:11.935650561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489446043Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489378395Z","close_reason":"AsciicastRecorder implemented with v2 format, NDJSON output, input/output recording, JSON escaping, and AsciicastLoader for playback (20 tests passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2fx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:18.050368656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:41.094181203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING ## Background Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics. ## Technical Requirements ### Tracing Infrastructure - Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging - Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE - Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline - Subscriber configuration for: - Development: pretty-printed to stderr - Testing: captured for assertions - Production: JSON structured output (optional) ### Key Instrumentation Points - Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete - Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time - Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level) - Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events - State changes: model updates, command execution - Error conditions: with full context ### Log Format Development: 2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15 2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10 Testing (structured capture): LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... } ### Feature Gates - feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation - feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production - Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead ## Design Rationale Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines.
